explanation: Datasheet table claims 96.1% efficiency at 50% load, but footnote 3 on page 8 states 'Efficiency reduces to 95.1% under VFI mode when harmonic filters are active at 50% load', which is below the 96% spec requirement.

Vendor submittal proposes N+1 module configuration (5 active, 1 standby) which complies with the Design Basis Report but violates Section 26 33 53 Part 2.1.2.A which mistakenly demands a minimum of N+2 redundancy.
Vendor submittal states compliance with IEC 62040-2 (EMC requirements) but does not provide certification under IEC 62040-3 (performance and test requirements) as explicitly mandated.
Submittal transmittal cover sheet states the submittal is reviewed against spec revision 'Rev 2020', but the active project specification is 'Rev 2024'.
Bypass static switch transfer time (spec required <= 4ms) is completely missing from the vendor datasheet parameter table.
UPS module overload rating is claimed as 125% for 5 minutes. Spec requires 125% overload capability for a minimum of 10 minutes.
Datasheet shows cooling airflow path as rear-exhaust. Spec requires a front-inlet and top-exhaust airflow configuration for hot/cold aisle containment.
UPS enclosure paint finish is listed as RAL 9005 (Jet Black). Spec requires RAL 7035 (Light Grey) polyester powder coating.
Generator standby rating of 2500 kVA is quoted at a reference ambient of 40°C on the datasheet cover. The spec mandates ratings be verified at the project design basis ambient of 45°C.

At 45°C design ambient, the Deccan generator derating curve (Figure 2, page 12) applies a -2.0% derating factor to the standby rating, resulting in an active capacity of 2450 kVA, which is below the 2500 kVA spec requirement.
Submittal states the generator controller utilizes the 'EMCP 3.2' control system. The spec mandates the current 'EMCP 4.4' controller, rendering this an obsolete hardware version.

Spec clause demands 'adequate ventilation' in the room. Submittal states 'room ventilation is adequate', which is a qualitative, unverifiable statement and does not submit air flow calculations. A deterministic verifier cannot rule on this.

Vendor submittal day tank has a maximum capacity of 990 Liters, providing only 1.9 hours of autonomy at full load. This violates spec 2.3.8.C requiring 2.0 hours (1040 L) and is linked to the code conflict spec defect DEF-007.
Fuel consumption rate at 50% load is omitted from the generator performance table on page 6 of the submittal.
Generator uses a single 24VDC electric starter motor. Spec requires dual starter motors in redundant configuration with independent starter batteries.
Datasheet shows sound level at 1 meter is 88 dBA in footnote 1. Spec requires maximum 85 dBA sound pressure level in a free field at 1 meter.
CRAH unit airflow is 62,390 m3/h, which is 3.0% below the spec requirement of 64,320 m3/h, violating the cooling-airflow basis.

Vendor submittal claims a COP of 5.3, matching the required COP of 5.2, but fails to state the chilled water entering/leaving temperatures or the air wet/dry bulb temperatures at which this COP is achieved, leaving the claim unverifiable. A deterministic verifier cannot rule on this.
CRAH fan motor power is listed as '7.5 HP' in the datasheet. The spec requires motor power to be defined in electrical kilowatts (kW) (conversion: 7.5 HP * 0.746 = 5.6 kW).
CRAH cooling coil utilizes R-407C refrigerant, but spec clause 2.2.8 explicitly mandates R-410A refrigerant for compliance with environmental policies.
The valve flow coefficient (Cv) of the 2-way modulating control valve is missing from the CRAH component table.
Vendor submittal specifies an infrared humidifier system. The specification explicitly mandates an electrode steam generating humidifier for humidity control.
CRAH is provided with a standard 2-way modulating control valve. Spec requires a pressure independent control valve (PICV) for chilled water flow regulation.
CRAH unit overall cabinet width is 2450 mm. The spec mandates a maximum cabinet width of 2400 mm to ensure transport clearance in corridors.
The vendor submittal package does not contain the required UL/FM factory test and compliance certificate for the clean agent cylinder valves.
Submittal states compliance with NFPA 2001 (2008 Edition). The project specification requires NFPA 2001 (2018 Edition), which contains critical safety amendments regarding agent hold times.

Main data table claims a 9.2s discharge time, but footnote 2 on page 5 reveals that 'discharge time extends to 11.0 seconds when piping runs exceed 45 meters', violating the NFPA 2001 maximum limit of 10 seconds.
Vendor compliance matrix states the agent hold time (retention) is 9 minutes (540 seconds) under worst-case room leakage, failing the spec minimum requirement of 10 minutes (600 seconds).
The NOAEL safety margin calculation is missing from the vendor submittal package.
Submittal provides a 360 psi ECS system. Spec requires 500 psi ECS-500 system configuration for optimal high-pressure gas distribution.

Total clean agent quantity detailed in the bill of materials is 11,800 kg. Design calculations in the specification mandate a minimum of 12,500 kg of agent to maintain design concentration across all zones.
Submittal compliance matrix notes that cylinders are certified to ISO 9809-1 only. The specification mandates certification under UL 2166 and FM 5600 standards.
Revised submittal VoltEdge UPS R1 shows an input THDi of 5.2% at full load, which exceeds the specified limit of 5.0% (introduced as a new deviation in R1).
Under Addendum 3, the UPS efficiency requirement was increased to 96.5%. VoltEdge UPS R1 efficiency at 100% load is 96.2%, failing this updated specification.
Under Addendum 3, the UPS efficiency requirement was increased to 96.5%. VoltEdge UPS R1 efficiency at 75% load is 96.0%, failing this updated specification.
Under Addendum 3, the UPS efficiency requirement was increased to 96.5%. VoltEdge UPS R1 efficiency at 50% load is 96.2%, failing this updated specification.
Bypass static switch transfer time remains missing in the R1 submittal package.
UPS module overload rating remains claimed as 125% for 5 minutes, failing the spec minimum of 10 minutes.
R1 submittal still specifies a rear-exhaust airflow configuration, violating the top-exhaust layout requirement.
R1 submittal still lists RAL 9005 (Jet Black) paint finish, violating the RAL 7035 (Light Grey) specification.
Vendor datasheet claims nominal capacity is 550 kWh, which when converted (550,000 Wh / 480 V = 1145.8 Ah) is below the required 1200 Ah, indicating a capacity shortfall of 54.2 Ah (4.5%).

Datasheet claims 10-minute battery runtime is rated at a load power factor of 0.9 PF. The spec requires battery runtime to be rated at a load power factor of 1.0 PF (unity), which represents a larger active power draw.
Battery cabinet DC bus voltage is listed as 400 VDC. The spec requires a 480 VDC nominal bus voltage to match the UPS inverter requirements.
The battery shelf life (required to be >= 6 months without charge) is missing from the battery cabinet specification sheet.
Fully loaded battery cabinet weight is listed as 3200 kg. Spec limits the maximum floor-load weight per cabinet to 2800 kg to prevent exceeding structural slab limits.
Battery cabinet datasheet lists operating temperature of 0°C to 20°C. Spec requires full battery warranty and operating parameters to hold from 0°C to 25°C ambient.
Battery cabinet is configured for bottom cable entry only. Spec requires dual entry (top or bottom cable entry conduits) to match overhead cable trays.
Low Voltage Disconnect (LVD) contactor rating is omitted from the battery cabinet component details.
Switchgear rated voltage is 12 kV, which meets the specified minimum of 12 kV.
Switchgear main busbar current rating is 2000 A, which meets the specified minimum of 2000 A.
Short-circuit withstand is 31.5 kA, which meets the specified minimum of 31.5 kA.
Short-circuit duration rating is 3 seconds, which meets the specified minimum of 3 seconds.
Enclosure IP rating is IP4X, which meets the specified minimum IP4X.
Internal Arc Classification is AFLR, which meets the specified classification of AFLR.
Switchgear includes ASTA/KEMA type test certificates for compliance with IEC 62271-200.
Rated short-circuit making capacity is 66 kA peak, meeting the specified minimum 66 kA.
Lightning impulse withstand voltage (BIL) is 75 kV, matching the specified 75 kV requirement.
Busbar material is high conductivity copper, meeting the specification.
Earthing busbar is copper and sized for full short circuit rating, meeting the spec.
Current transformers have metering accuracy class 0.5s and protection class 5P20, meeting spec.
Voltage transformers have accuracy class 0.5, meeting the spec.
Switchgear operating temperature range is -5°C to 45°C, meeting design basis.
Loss of service continuity is LSC-2B, which meets LSC-2B specification.
UPS module capacity is 1200 kW, which meets the specified minimum of 1200 kW.
UPS module has a unity power factor (1.0), matching the specified 1.0 PF.
UPS input THD is 4.2% at full load, which meets the specified maximum of 5.0%.
UPS module double-conversion efficiency at 100% load is 96.2%, meeting the specified minimum of 96.0%.
UPS module double-conversion efficiency at 75% load is 96.0%, meeting the specified minimum of 96.0%.
Generator power factor is 0.8, which meets the specified 0.8 PF.
Generator output voltage is 11 kV, matching the specified 11 kV system nominal voltage.
Generator speed is 1500 RPM, matching the specified 1500 RPM for 50Hz operation.
Generator accepts 100% block load in a single step, meeting the ISO 8528-5 G3 performance class.
Generator uses electronic governor class G3 per ISO 8528-5, meeting the spec.
CRAH unit sensible cooling capacity is 250 kW, which meets the specified minimum of 250 kW.
CRAH entering water temperature claimed is 10°C, matching pre-addendum spec of 10°C, but violating post-addendum spec of 11°C.
CRAH leaving water temperature is 18°C, matching the design basis 18°C leaving chilled water.
Fan power density is 0.09 W/(m3/h), which meets the specified limit of < 0.10 W/(m3/h).
CRAH operating relative humidity range is 20% to 80% non-condensing, meeting spec.
Suppressant agent is FK-5-1-12, matching the specified FK-5-1-12 clean agent.
Design concentration is 4.7% (v/v), which matches the specified design basis.
Design concentration of 4.7% is below the NOAEL safety threshold of 10.0% (v/v), providing an acceptable safety margin.
UPS module capacity is 1200 kW, meeting the 1200 kW requirement.
UPS module operates at unity power factor, meeting spec.
R1 submittal now specifies performance compliance and testing certifications per IEC 62040-3.
R1 submittal transmittal cover sheet correctly cites active spec revision 'Rev 2024'.
Battery capacity is quoted as '576 kWh' in the submittal. Converting this (576,000 Wh / 480 V = 1200 Ah) matches the required capacity of 1200 Ah.
Design Basis says UPS N+1, but Section 26 33 53 Part 2.1.2.A states 'UPS system modules shall be configured in a minimum N+2 parallel redundant configuration'.

UPS spec Section 26 33 53 Part 2.3.1.E requires a 10-min battery runtime, but its own Part 3.4.2.C Field testing acceptance criterion says 'inverter shall maintain full 100% IT load for a minimum of 12 minutes on battery power'.

Common Electrical Section 26 05 00 Part 1.4.3.B mandates IEC test certificates for all medium voltage gear, but Section 26 13 26 Part 1.2.1 references UL standards only for certification, causing a conflict.

CRAH cooling unit COP/efficiency is specified as 'minimum COP of 5.2' in Part 2.2.4.C with no operating conditions (no entering/leaving water temperatures or airflow speed specified), making the performance claim unverifiable.
Generator room clause Part 3.2.1.F mandates 'adequate ventilation shall be provided in the generator room to prevent heat buildup,' which is an unverifiable qualitative requirement.
Clean Agent Fire Suppression spec Section 21 22 00 Part 1.2.1 references 'NFPA 2001 (2008 Edition)', which is a withdrawn standard revision.

Section 26 32 13 Part 2.3.8.C demands generator day tanks support >= 2.0 hours of runtime at full load (1040 L), which directly conflicts with local NBC fire safety codes capping indoor fuel day tanks at 1000 L.
